altIdentifier.idno: Not in SC (late accession): no description available

msItem.title: Expositio hymnorum Hilarii siue Aurea expositio hymnorum
msItem.note: On the text see Helmut Gneuss, Hymnar und Hymnen im englischen Mittelalter (1968), ch. 9 'Die Expositio Hymnorum'. This copy closely resembles the early printed texts (Gneuss's "Typ P", e.g. Paris, 1487, GW 13688) except that after 'A solis ortus', fol. 10r, near the end of the first scribe's stint, follow (for Nativity or Epiphany) 'Salvator mundi domine' (with a later marginal note 'vacat') and the beginning only of 'A patre unigenitus', the text left unfinished at the end of fol. 10r. The second scribe's stint begins after a gap mid-way through fol. 10v with 'Hostis Herodes', omiting the hymns for Lent found in the printed texts. The final hymns are 'Ihesu corona virginum' and 'Virginis proles opifexque'.

The hymns included are therefore: Primo dierum omnium, Eterne rerum conditor, Nocte surgentes, Ecce iam noctis, Iam lucis orto, Nunc sancte nobis, Rector potens, Rerum deus tenax, Lucis creator, Te lucis ante terminum, Christe qui lux es, Somno refectis, Splendor paterne, Immense celi conditor, Consors paterni luminis, Ales diei nuncius, Telluris ingens conditor, Rerum creator optime, Nox et tenebre, Celi deus sanctissime, Nox atra rerum, Lux ecce surgit, Magne deus potencie, Tu trinitatis unitas, Eterna celi gloria, Plasmator hominis, Summe deus clementie, Aurora iam spargit, O lux beata trinitas, Conditor alme siderum, Verbum supernum prodiens, Vox data, Veni redemptor gencium, Christe redemptor omnium (sic), A solis ortus, A patre unigenitus, Salvator mundi domine, (fol. 10v) Hostis Herodes, Ave maris stella, Quem terra pontus, O gloriosa domina, Quod chorus vatum, Audi benigne conditor,
Vexilla regis prodeunt, Pange lingua, Lustra sex, Rex eterne domine, Aurora lucis rutilat, Ad cenam agni, Eterne rex altissime, Ihesu nostra redemcio (without initial), Veni creator spiritus, Iam Christus astra, Beata nobis gaudia, Ut queant laxis (margin: M' sancti Iohannis babtiste), Omnis felix meritique, Felix per omnes, O Roma felix, Aurea luce, Doctor egregie, Tibi Christe splendor, Christe sanctorum decus, Christe redemptor omnium, Ihesu saluator seculi, Rex Christe Martini, Martine par apostolis, Exultet celum laudibus, Eterna Christi munera, Sanctorum meritis inclita, Iste confessor domini, Rex gloriose martirum, Martir dei qui, Ihesu corona virginum, Virginis proles opifexque.
From fol. 16v (Eterne rex altissime) the accessus are no longer given.
Two hymns added, 14th century: 'Hymnum canamus glorie' (Cantus 008316, fol. 16v), 'Adest beato matyri' (AH 19, p. 255, fol. 26v).

layoutDesc.layout: 1 col. of c. 64 lines, the hymn texts in a larger module on alternate lines; ruled space c. 155 c. 75 Fols. 1r-10r ruled more elaborately with additional divisions in the upper, lower and outer margins.

handDesc.handNote: Textualis (Gothic bookhand), two scribes, fols. 1r-10r and 10v-26v.

musicNotation.p: Alphabetical notation to about thirty hymns.

history.provenance: A. C. de la Mare, 'bought from Rosenthal, early 1970s'.
history.acquisition: Bequeathed by her to the Bodleian.

recordHist.source: Preliminary description by Matthew Holford, May 2026.

availability.p: This item is currently being housed and requires curatorial permission to consult. To request consultation please email specialcollections.enquiries@bodleian.ox.ac.uk.
